text: Add release / publish workflow (PyPI, GitHub Releases) once a 1.0 release is ready. Out of scope for 011-S which is CI-gate bootstrap only.

[Docling OCR tuning, plan-review F3 from 015-S] Enable `PdfPipelineOptions(do_ocr=True)` in `_read_pdf_docling_pages` to extract text from scanned/image-only PDFs that the current pipeline returns empty for. Defaults to docling's bundled OCR engine (likely Tesseract or EasyOCR). Lower priority because the current heuristic baseline also produces no text for scanned PDFs â€” enabling OCR is an enhancement, not a regression fix. Closure: docs/closure/015-S-post-g3-hygiene.md.

[Spike] Identify maximum safe PDF size / page count for docling layout model. Load test on 2026-06-04 crashed docling rt_detr layout model with `RuntimeError: [enforce fail at alloc_cpu.cpp:117] DefaultCPUAllocator: not enough memory: you tried to allocate 6553600 bytes` while processing azure-cosmos-db.pdf (~554-part output). The `--pdf-engine auto` fallback only catches `PdfReadError`, NOT `RuntimeError`/`MemoryError`, so the entire batch aborted with no output emitted. Spike scope: (a) characterize docling rt_detr peak working-set memory growth as a function of page count, total document bytes, and image-richness, on representative target hardware; (b) determine a conservative safety threshold (e.g. "â‰¤ N pages or â‰¤ M MB or â‰¤ K images") above which docline must split the PDF before invoking docling; (c) recommend whether `auto` engine should also catch `RuntimeError` / `OSError` / `MemoryError` and fall back to heuristic; (d) recommend whether to add an upfront pre-flight size/page-count check in `_resolve_layout_engine` that downgrades to heuristic before docling is invoked. Unblocks splitter (item 2) and batch processor (item 3). Spike output: docs/decisions/{date}-spike-docling-pdf-size-threshold.md with measured numbers, recommended threshold, and recommended fallback policy. Linked: docs/closure/015-S-post-g3-hygiene.md (engine resolution rules) and docs/closure/014-S-docling-sidecars.md (auto fallback in read_pdf_pages).

[Feature, blocked-by spike] PDF splitter for oversized PDFs that exceed the docling-safe threshold from item 1. Module placement: src/docline/readers/pdf_splitter.py. API: `split_pdf(path: Path, max_pages: int, page_overlap: int = 0) -> list[Path]` returning paths to a deterministic, hashed sub-PDF set staged under a temp/cache directory. Requirements: (a) lossless page extraction using a stable library (pypdf preferred since it's already in the dependency graph; fall back to pikepdf if pypdf can't round-trip embedded forms or annotations correctly); (b) split unit is page count, not byte count â€” the docling memory pressure is driven by page count Ă— image density, not file size; (c) deterministic chunk identifiers (e.g. `{source_hash}-chunk-NNNN.pdf`) so re-runs reuse cached splits; (d) preserve original PDF metadata (title, author, creation date) in each chunk frontmatter for downstream provenance reconstruction; (e) support `page_overlap` argument (default 0) â€” when > 0, each chunk after the first includes the last N pages of the prior chunk to enable header-group reconstruction in item 3; (f) emit a manifest sidecar mapping chunk index â†’ original page range so the batch processor can stitch outputs back to original document coordinates. Tests: unit tests on a fixture corpus (small PDF that splits cleanly, large PDF with embedded forms, image-heavy PDF). No CLI surface yet â€” the splitter is invoked by item 3 (batch processor). Wires into: src/docline/elt/execute.py (post-fetch, pre-process), src/docline/readers/pdf.py (skip docling for already-split chunks since they're under threshold). Closure: docs/closure/{shipment}-pdf-splitter.md.

[Feature, depends-on splitter] Batch processor for split PDFs with page overlap and post-split graph stitching for graphtor ingestion. Module placement: src/docline/process/pdf_batch.py. Workflow: (1) detect when a source PDF exceeds the docling-safe threshold (from item 1); (2) invoke the splitter (item 2) with a configurable `page_overlap` (default likely 2-4 pages, tunable via env or process config); (3) process each chunk independently through docling (now safe because each chunk is under threshold); (4) reconcile the per-chunk outputs by detecting overlapping header groups (H1/H2 anchors that appear in both chunk N's tail and chunk N+1's head) and deduplicating; (5) emit a single logical document output with continuous part-NNNN.md numbering AND a `docline:` namespace key `split_chunks` recording the original chunk boundaries so graphtor can verify stitching integrity. Requirements: (a) chunk boundary detection must be heading-anchor-aware (use existing chunk-anchor IDs from 013-S referentiality); (b) overlap reconciliation must be deterministic â€” if the same H1 appears in two overlapping chunks, the SECOND occurrence is dropped, not the first, so the first occurrence's frontmatter wins; (c) media files from each chunk must be remapped to the unified document's media/ directory with chunk-prefix to avoid collision (e.g. `media/c0001-figure-0001.png`); (d) the unified manifest entry replaces the per-chunk entries â€” graphtor consumes one logical document, not 5 chunks; (e) failure-of-one-chunk policy: if chunk K fails, downgrade THAT chunk to heuristic, do not abort the batch (this is the inverse of the bug that took down the cosmos PDF load test). Tests: integration test on a synthetic large PDF that exercises overlap reconciliation and chunk-level fallback. Closure: docs/closure/{shipment}-pdf-batch-stitching.md.
